Class ServerStats

java.lang.Object
com.common.ServerStats
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class ServerStats extends Object implements Serializable
Contains server statistics sent back to clients in response to HEALTH requests. This class is Serializable so it can be sent over TCP. Provides information about: - Server uptime (how long server has been running) - Memory usage (free and total) - Number of active client connections - Size of the music band collection - Server start time
See Also:

  • Constructor Details

    • ServerStats

      public ServerStats()
      Default constructor required for deserialization.

    • ServerStats

      public ServerStats(long uptimeMillis, long freeMemory, long totalMemory, int activeConnections, int collectionSize, LocalDateTime startTime)
      Constructor with all statistics.
      Parameters:
      uptimeMillis - How long server has been running in ms
      freeMemory - Free memory in bytes
      totalMemory - Total memory in bytes
      activeConnections - Number of connected clients
      collectionSize - Number of music bands
      startTime - When server started

  • Method Details

    • getUptimeFormatted

      public String getUptimeFormatted()
      Formats uptime as human-readable string. Example outputs: "1d 2h 3m 4s", "2h 30m 15s", "45s"
      Returns:
      Formatted uptime string

    • getMemoryFormatted

      public String getMemoryFormatted()
      Formats memory as human-readable string. Example output: "256MB / 512MB"
      Returns:
      Formatted memory string
